content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
October 30,
1992
Kay Keck,
CMC
City Clerk’s Office
P.O.
Box 2340
Dublin,
CA
94568
RE:
Your Request For Informal
Assistance
Our File No.
1—92-527
Dear Ms.
Keck:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”) .1/
Because your request is general in nature,
we are
treating your letter as a request for informal assistance pursuant
to the provisions of Regulation 18329.2/
You have asked a number of questions concerning campaign
filing deadlines in connection with the January 5,
1993, city
election.
The Act provides that when a filing deadline for a pre—
election or semi—annual campaign statement falls on a Saturday,
Sunday or official state holiday, the filing deadline is extended
to the next regular business day.
(FPPC Regulation 18116.)
The first filing deadline in connection with this election
falls on November 26,
1992,
Thanksgiving Day,
an “official” state
holiday.
Therefore,
the first pre—election statement will cover
the period January
1
(or July 1,
for candidates/officeholders who
were required to file a campaign statement(s)
covering the period
January
1 through June 30,
1992)
through November 21,
1992 and is
due November 27,
1992.
The deadline for filing the second pre
election campaign statement falls on December 24,
1992,
covering
the period November
22 through December 19,
1992.
The
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000,
et g.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Section
18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800
• P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807
•
(916) 322-5660


Kay Keck
Our File No.
1—92—527
Page
2
semi-annual campaign statement is due August 2,
1993
(July 31,
1993, is a Saturday so the deadline will be extended to the next
business day)
covering the period December 20,
1992 through June
30,
1993.
Your letter states that city offices will be closed on
November 27th, the filing deadline for the first pre—election
statement and December 24th, the filing deadline for the second
pre—election statement.
However,
because these dates are not
“official state holidays,” the deadlines may not be extended.
Section 84200.8 requires the second pre—election statement to
be sent by guaranteed overnight delivery service or delivered in
person.
Therefore, candidates and committees will be required to
file the statement due on December 24 by personal delivery prior
to the due date or, if sent by guaranteed overnight delivery
service, the delivery service must receive the statement on or
before the due date.
For purposes of determining when the
statement has been filed, the statement is considered filed on the
date it is received by the delivery service and not the date the
filing officer receives the statement.
Reports personally
delivered by a candidate or committee are considered received by
the filing officer on the date of delivery.
Section 81007 provides that statements sent by first-class
mail are deemed to have been received by the filing officer on the
date of deposit in the mail.
First pre-election statements and
semi—annual statements may be sent by first class mail or
personally delivered.
Your letter further states that with regard to the 24-hour
late contribution/late independent expenditure reporting
requirements during the last 16 days before the election, nine of
these 16 days will fall on either weekends or holidays.
Sections 84203 and 84204 require that late contributions and
late independent expenditures be reported by mailgraiu,3/ telegram,
3/ Effective January 1,
1993, mailgram will no longer be an
acceptable means of filing late contribution reports or late
independent expenditure reports.
These reports must be sent by
telegram, guaranteed overnight delivery or delivered in person.
Faxed copies are permitted if the filing officer is willing to
accept faxed transmissions.


Kay Kec]c
Our File No.
1-92-527
Page
3
guaranteed overnight delivery service,
or delivered in person
within 24 hours.
The Act does not contain provisions for
extending this deadline.
Late contribution reports and late
independent expenditure reports are required to be sent within 24
hours of making or receiving a late contribution or making a late
independent expenditure.
This means that the report must be
received by the delivery service or Western Union within 24 hours
of the time the contribution is received or made or the
expenditure is made.
The date filed is not when the city clerk’s
office receives the report, but the date received by the delivery
service or Western Union.
Reports personally delivered by a
candidate or committee are considered received by the filing
officer on the date of delivery.
If you have any questions concerning this matter, please
contact me at 916/322-5662.
Sincerely
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question: You have asked a number of questions concerning campaign filing deadlines in connection with the January 5, 1993, city election.
sections.conclusion: The Act provides that when a filing deadline for a pre-election or semi-annual campaign statement falls on a Saturday, Sunday or official state holiday, the filing deadline is extended to the next regular business day. (FPPC Regulation 18116.) Because city office closures on November 27th and December 24th are not 'official state holidays,' the deadlines may not be extended. The Act does not contain provisions for extending the 24-hour late contribution/late independent expenditure reporting deadlines, which must be reported within 24 hours regardless of weekends or holidays.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the correct campaign filing deadlines for the January 5, 1993 Dublin city election, particularly when deadlines fall on holidays or weekends, and how do the 24-hour late contribution/late independent expenditure reporting requirements apply when those deadlines occur on non-business days?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Filing deadlines are extended only for official state holidays, not local office closures, so November 27 and December 24 deadlines stand as scheduled. The 24-hour late contribution and late independent expenditure reporting requirements have no holiday extensions and must be met within 24 hours regardless of when that period falls on weekends or holidays.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard informal advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. Document provides detailed statutory and regulatory references. Footnote 2 notes this is informal assistance without immunity protection under Government Code Section 83114.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have asked a number of questions concerning campaign filing deadlines in connection with the January 5, 1993, city election.

CONCLUSION: The Act provides that when a filing deadline for a pre-election or semi-annual campaign statement falls on a Saturday, Sunday or official state holiday, the filing deadline is extended to the next regular business day. (FPPC Regulation 18116.) Because city office closures on November 27th and December 24th are not 'official state holidays,' the deadlines may not be extended. The Act does not contain provisions for extending the 24-hour late contribution/late independent expenditure reporting deadlines, which must be reported within 24 hours regardless of weekends or holidays.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to Dublin's City Clerk regarding campaign filing deadlines for the January 5, 1993 election, clarifying deadline extensions for holidays and the application of 24-hour late contribution/expenditure reporting requirements when they fall on non-business days.
